commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining blocker is maintainer approval plus current-head real behavior proof; an automated repair should not choose the public config/API or trust-boundary direction.; Needs attention: The diff introduces a maintainer-visible trust-boundary decision by letting memory-role slot selection expose plugin hooks and skills. commands.review_summary:
The PR adds global and per-agent purpose-specific memory role slots, legacy memory-slot migration/diagnostics, role-aware plugin activation/runtime paths, docs, and focused tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature PR; source inspection confirms current main still has only the single `plugins.slots.memory` contract while this branch adds the new role-slot surfaces.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is maintainer approval plus current-head real behavior proof; an automated repair should not choose the public config/API or trust-boundary direction.

target.title: feat(memory): add multi-slot memory role architecture
target.body:
## Summary

This PR adds a purpose-specific memory role-slot contract so memory plugins can compose instead of replacing each other through one global `plugins.slots.memory` selector.

What changes:

- Adds canonical memory role slots: `memory.recall`, `memory.compaction`, `memory.capture`, `memory.dreaming`, and `memory.userModel`.
- Keeps legacy `plugins.slots.memory` as a backward-compatible recall shorthand during an extended migration window.
- Adds per-agent memory role-slot overrides under `agents.list[*].plugins.slots`, limited to memory roles plus legacy `memory`.
- Wires role-slot selection through config schema/types/help/docs, plugin activation, gateway startup preloading, runtime memory resolution, skill publishing, validation, doctor repair/preview, uninstall cleanup, status diagnostics, and SDK-facing aliases.
- Preserves the existing global `plugins.slots.contextEngine` path for LosslessClaw and other context-engine plugins.
- Explicitly rejects unsupported per-agent `agents.list[*].plugins.slots.contextEngine` overrides, because agent-scoped context-engine semantics are out of scope for this PR.

Why this matters:

- Advanced OpenClaw users are already operating layered memory stacks: factual recall, context compaction, auto-capture, dreaming/consolidation, and inferred user modeling are distinct concerns.
- The previous single memory slot forced useful plugins into workarounds such as context-engine ownership, sidecars, or global replacement of the primary recall plugin.
- This branch makes the config contract match the memory architecture users are trying to run while keeping the migration path conservative.

Intended outcome:

- `memory-core` can remain the factual recall owner while other plugins own capture, compaction, dreaming, or user-model roles.
- Existing configs using `plugins.slots.memory` continue to work.
- `openclaw doctor --fix` copies non-conflicting legacy `plugins.slots.memory` values into `plugins.slots["memory.recall"]` but keeps the legacy key in place for plugin compatibility.
- Plugin lifecycle and diagnostics treat role-slot-selected memory plugins as intentionally active across startup, validation, skill publishing, runtime resolution, and cleanup.

Out of scope:

- This does not replace or remove `plugins.slots.contextEngine`; LosslessClaw and other context engines continue to use the existing global `contextEngine` slot.
- This does not add agent-scoped `contextEngine` semantics.
- This does not implement the owner-provenance object shape proposed in #70823.
- This does not require third-party plugins to implement every memory role immediately.

## Linked context

Closes #60572.

Directly related memory-composition requests and prior proposals:

- #38874: supplementary/additive memory plugins alongside the primary memory slot.
- #2306: earlier closed PR proposing stackable memory plugins.
- #2080: earlier request for multiple simultaneous memory plugins and emerging memory architectures.
- #77714: pluggable persistent memory backend demand.

Dreaming and memory-role separation context:

- #85473: future protocol work for making dreaming implementable by non-`memory-core` memory plugins. This PR adds the `memory.dreaming` slot contract but does not implement that full protocol.
- #63067 and #65411: prior work around keeping dreaming behavior available when another plugin owns the legacy memory slot.
- #62275: previous selected-slot dreaming config behavior.

Runtime/diagnostic slot handling context:

- #76567 and #76495: configured memory-slot runtime checks should load/consult the selected memory plugin.
- #78540: doctor/status memory diagnostics could misreport configured memory providers.
- #76550: stale `plugins.slots.*` references could leave config unrecoverable until doctor cleanup was broadened.

Context-engine compatibility context:

- #77063, #77185, #76576, and #81843: context-engine registration and LosslessClaw-adjacent failures shaped the compatibility review for preserving the existing global `plugins.slots.contextEngine` behavior while rejecting unsupported per-agent `contextEngine` config.

Future API/RFC context:

- #70823: future machine-readable slot ownership/provenance. This PR intentionally keeps scalar plugin IDs and solves the memory role composition layer first.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Current-head proof refresh for PR #86210 at `bb771d08911d361241d402bf684ca41645623f02`, covering the multi-slot memory role contract, legacy `plugins.slots.memory` migration into `memory.recall`, per-agent memory role override behavior, authenticated GitHub Copilot model availability through OpenClaw, and Honcho availability on the [REDACTED] proof host.
- Real environment tested: Current-head deterministic/upgrade proof ran on [REDACTED] proof host `[REDACTED]` via `[REDACTED]`, checkout `[REDACTED]`, Node `v22.22.2`, pnpm `11.2.2`, artifact `[REDACTED]`. Current-head authenticated live model and Honcho proof ran on `[REDACTED]` via `[REDACTED]`, same checkout detached to `bb771d08911d361241d402bf684ca41645623f02`, artifact `[REDACTED]`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: On `[REDACTED]`, fetched `[REDACTED]`, detached to `bb771d08911d361241d402bf684ca41645623f02`, then ran `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`, `corepack pnpm build:strict-smoke`, focused plugin/config/doctor/runtime suites, agentic shard, `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json`, `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json`, `git diff --check`, old-base and PR-head config/doctor migration harnesses, and `openclaw models status --probe --json`. On `[REDACTED]`, detached the proof checkout to the same head and ran `node openclaw.mjs models status --probe --json`, `node openclaw.mjs infer model run --local --model github-copilot/gpt-5-mini --prompt "Reply exactly: OPENCLAW_PR86210_BB771D_NANO_OK" --json`, `node openclaw.mjs honcho status`, and `node openclaw.mjs honcho ask "For proof, reply with exactly LOCAL_HONCHO_BB771D_OK."`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Redacted runtime logs are preserved on the proof hosts at the artifact paths above; maintainers without host access can use the copied redacted excerpts below. GitHub proof gate run for the body format: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26554411901/job/78223133299.

```text
[REDACTED] redacted/01-models-status.log
"defaultModel": "github-copilot/gpt-5-mini"
"resolvedDefault": "github-copilot/gpt-5-mini"
"providersWithOAuth": ["github-copilot (1)"]
"github-copilot:github=token:REDACTED"
"provider": "github-copilot", "model": "github-copilot/gpt-5-mini", "status": "ok"
```

```json
[REDACTED] redacted/02-direct-model.log
{
  "ok": true,
  "capability": "model.run",
  "transport": "local",
  "provider": "github-copilot",
  "model": "gpt-5-mini",
  "outputs": [{ "text": "OPENCLAW_PR86210_BB771D_NANO_OK", "mediaUrl": null }]
}
```

```text
[REDACTED] redacted/05-honcho-status.log
Connected to Honcho
  Workspace: [REDACTED]

[REDACTED] redacted/06-honcho-ask.log
LOCAL_HONCHO_BB771D_OK
```

```text
[REDACTED] redacted/18-pr-head-minimal-doctor-fix-validate.log
plugins.slots.memory: copied legacy memory slot to memory.recall (memory-core) and kept the legacy key for plugin compatibility.
plugins.slots.memory: legacy memory slot remains for plugin compatibility; use memory.recall as the canonical selector.
{"valid":true,"path":"[REDACTED]"}
{"legacy":"memory-core","recall":"memory-core"}
```

```text
[REDACTED] redacted/10-plugin-memory-tests.log
Test Files 5 passed (5)
Tests 320 passed (320)
```

```text
[REDACTED] command-summary.json
"deterministicOk": true
"deterministicFailures": []
required gates included 10-plugin-memory-tests, 11-command-config-memory-tests, 12-extension-memory-tests, 15-pr-head-doctor-fix-validate-fixed, 18-pr-head-minimal-doctor-fix-validate, and 19-models-status-probe.
```

- Observed result after fix: The current head validates legacy memory-slot upgrade behavior by copying `plugins.slots.memory = "memory-core"` into `plugins.slots["memory.recall"] = "memory-core"` while retaining the legacy key for third-party plugin compatibility. Focused memory-role coverage passed `5` plugin/config/runtime files and `320` tests, including per-agent memory-slot routing paths. [REDACTED] proved the configured GitHub Copilot provider profile is present and usable by OpenClaw, direct inference returned the exact marker `OPENCLAW_PR86210_BB771D_NANO_OK`, Honcho was connected to workspace `[REDACTED]`, and `honcho ask` returned `LOCAL_HONCHO_BB771D_OK`.
- What was not tested: Current-head gateway live-suite selected-model execution is not claimed as passing in this proof. The direct gateway live harness needed a temporary clean config because the suite temp home does not install non-bundled `openclaw-honcho`; with explicit `OPENCLAW_LIVE_GATEWAY_MODELS=github-copilot/gpt-5-mini`, the current registry selection still reported `selected no explicit live models ... from 13 registry model(s)`. The older [REDACTED] gateway suite proof at `e628671e1a77421c5e962c5182b44f71dc5b97b8` is preserved as historical evidence but is not claimed as exact-head proof here.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The `[REDACTED]` proof host did not have the same live auth/Honcho availability as [REDACTED], so authenticated live model and Honcho proof were refreshed on [REDACTED] at the exact current PR head. Local artifact paths are host-visible paths, so the PR body includes copied redacted excerpts for maintainer-visible review.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): The previous body incorrectly stated that the refreshed [REDACTED] run lacked usable live auth/profile targets and Honcho availability. Current-head [REDACTED] evidence above shows `github-copilot:github` profile status `ok`, direct `github-copilot/gpt-5-mini` inference success, Honcho connection, and Honcho ask success.

## Implementation notes

- Adds `src/plugins/slot-resolution.ts` as the shared role-slot helper layer for canonical memory role keys, defaults, normalization, effective per-agent resolution, and configured selection collection.
- Updates config schema/types/help/labels/docs for global and per-agent memory role slots.
- Keeps `PluginSlotsConfig.contextEngine` for global plugin slots, and adds an agent-slot type/schema that omits `contextEngine`.
- Updates doctor legacy memory-slot handling so `doctor --fix` canonicalizes to `memory.recall` while retaining legacy `memory` during the compatibility window.
- Updates plugin loader/activation/startup resolution so explicit role-slot selections activate memory plugins without confusing default `memory-core` fallback with a user-selected plugin under restrictive allowlists.
- Extends validation to report missing role-slot plugin refs and validate schemas for plugins selected only by non-recall or per-agent memory roles.
- Extends skill publishing, status scanning, uninstall cleanup, memory runtime, dreaming, compaction provider, and SDK alias behavior to use the role-aware model.
- Keeps memory runtime registrations addressable by plugin id, so runtime recall dispatch follows the resolved global or per-agent `memory.recall` owner.
- Passes the session agent id into Pi embedded extension factory construction so safeguard compaction provider selection honors per-agent `memory.compaction` overrides in both normal session runs and manual/queued compaction paths.
- Updates bundled memory plugin manifests/docs to describe role support and the recall shorthand migration.

## Compatibility and risk

Backward compatibility preserved:

- Existing `plugins.slots.memory` configs continue to resolve as recall selection.
- `doctor --fix` now keeps the legacy `plugins.slots.memory` key in place after copying to `memory.recall`, so third-party plugins that still read only the legacy key do not hit an immediate flag day.
- The default recall plugin remains `memory-core` unless explicitly disabled or overridden.
- `plugins.enabled = false`, denylist behavior, and explicit `plugins.entries.<id>.enabled = false` remain respected.
- Global `plugins.slots.contextEngine` remains separate from memory roles and continues to select context engines such as LosslessClaw.

Compatibility changes reviewers should inspect:

- `memory.recall`, `memory.compaction`, `memory.capture`, `memory.dreaming`, and `memory.userModel` become durable public config keys after merge.
- Per-agent plugin-slot overrides are memory-only. `agents.list[*].plugins.slots.contextEngine` is intentionally rejected until a future PR defines and implements agent-scoped context-engine semantics.
- Legacy `plugins.slots.memory` is still deprecated as a canonical authoring target even though it remains supported and retained for compatibility.

Primary risks:

- The public memory role taxonomy becomes API surface after merge, so naming and docs need maintainer signoff.
- Role-gating mistakes could attach the wrong memory provider or suppress expected memory behavior; this PR adds focused coverage across loader, validation, startup, runtime, doctor, status, skill publishing, and cleanup surfaces.
- Third-party plugins should eventually read `memory.recall`, but the retained legacy key avoids forcing that ecosystem update immediately.

## Verification

Current pushed head:

- `bb771d08911d361241d402bf684ca41645623f02` (`fix(dev): avoid Discord smoke retry after timeout`)

[REDACTED] proof gates from the refreshed head:

- `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`: passed.
- `corepack pnpm build:strict-smoke`: passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.plugins.config.ts src/plugins/slots.test.ts src/plugins/loader.test.ts src/plugins/memory-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/memory-state.test.ts src/plugins/config-state.test.ts src/plugins/channel-plugin-ids.test.ts src/plugins/uninstall.test.ts src/plugins/update.test.ts`: passed, 5 files / 320 tests.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.commands.config.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-memory-slot.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/stale-plugin-config.test.ts src/commands/status.scan.shared.test.ts`: passed, 1 file / 14 tests.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-active-memory.config.ts extensions/active-memory/index.test.ts`: passed, 1 file / 126 tests.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-memory.config.ts extensions/memory-wiki/src/bridge.test.ts`: passed, 1 file / 11 tests.
- Agentic shard: passed.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json`: passed.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json`: passed.
- `git diff --check`: passed.
- Old-base minimal legacy memory-slot config validation at `d2319d718c9c0340c750fc564532ffa9f5f1b7eb`: `config validate --json` returned `valid=true`; doctor lint then reported expected empty-temp-home gateway/auth/skills warnings.
- PR-head `doctor --fix --non-interactive` upgrade checks: passed; `config validate --json` returned `valid=true`; migration output retained `plugins.slots.memory = "memory-core"` and copied `plugins.slots["memory.recall"] = "memory-core"`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts test/scripts/dev-tooling-safety.test.ts`: passed, 1 file / 19 tests.
- `openclaw models status --probe --json`: completed and documented [REDACTED]'s current missing live model auth/profile state.

Current GitHub Actions signal for pushed head `bb771d08911d361241d402bf684ca41645623f02` at body refresh time:

- `Real behavior proof`: passed.
- `checks-node-core-fast`: passed.
- Most quality/security/build shards: passed.
- `check-lint`: failed in GitHub with one oxlint annotation in `scripts/check-cli-startup-memory.mjs:30`, but the same head passes local `corepack pnpm lint --threads=8`; GitHub refused targeted rerun with `workflow file may be broken`.
- `Security High (network-ssrf-boundary)`: cancelled after the 25 minute maximum execution time with no assertion failure annotation; GitHub refused targeted rerun with `workflow file may be broken`.

Live proof limitation:

- The previous body's [REDACTED] live model/gateway/Honcho proof was from stale head `e628671e1a77421c5e962c5182b44f71dc5b97b8`; it is intentionally not reused here. The refreshed [REDACTED] run could not complete authenticated live model, gateway, or Honcho proof because the host lacked live auth/profile targets and Honcho availability. The redacted logs preserve those negative availability checks.
